description: Data-driven prompt strategy registry. Add new strategies here — zero code changes required.

strategies.name: Structured Output Enforcement
strategies.description: Forces output into a specific JSON/structured format with schema guidance

template.system_addendum: You MUST respond in the exact JSON schema specified. Do not deviate from the schema. Validate your output mentally before responding.

strategies.name: Role Prompting (Enhanced)
strategies.description: Assigns a specific expert role beyond the base persona for domain-specific tasks

template.system_addendum: Embody this role fully. Draw on the specific domain expertise, clinical experience, and professional standards expected of this specialist. Your reasoning should reflect the depth of a seasoned practitioner.

strategies.name: Chain of Thought
strategies.description: Encourages step-by-step reasoning before arriving at a conclusion

template.prefix: Think through this step-by-step before providing your final answer:
template.suffix: Provide your reasoning chain, then your final conclusion.
template.system_addendum: Use chain-of-thought reasoning. Show each logical step explicitly before concluding.

source.paper: Wei et al. 2022 — Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models

strategies.name: Few-Shot Learning
strategies.description: Provides exemplar input-output pairs to guide response format and quality

template.system_addendum: Follow the format demonstrated in the examples below exactly.

source.paper: Brown et al. 2020 — Language Models are Few-Shot Learners

strategies.name: ReAct (Reason + Act)
strategies.description: Interleaves reasoning and action steps for tool-using agents

template.system_addendum: For each step, first state your Thought (reasoning about what to do next), then your Action (the specific action to take), then your Observation (what you learned). Continue this Thought-Action-Observation loop until you have a complete answer.

source.paper: Yao et al. 2022 — ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models

strategies.name: Self-Consistency
strategies.description: Generates multiple reasoning paths and selects the most consistent answer

template.system_addendum: Consider this problem from three different clinical perspectives. For each perspective, reason independently to a conclusion. Then synthesize the most consistent answer across all three reasoning paths.

source.paper: Wang et al. 2022 — Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning

strategies.name: Step-Back Prompting
strategies.description: First asks a high-level abstraction question before addressing the specific problem

template.system_addendum: Before answering the specific clinical question, first step back and consider: What are the general principles and pathophysiology relevant to this presentation? Then apply those principles to this specific case.

source.paper: Zheng et al. 2023 — Take a Step Back: Evoking Reasoning via Abstraction

strategies.name: Tree of Thought
strategies.description: Explores multiple reasoning branches, evaluating and pruning as it goes

template.system_addendum: Approach this as a tree of possible reasoning paths. Identify 2-3 candidate hypotheses. For each, evaluate the supporting and contradicting evidence. Prune unlikely branches and expand the most promising one to a final conclusion.

source.paper: Yao et al. 2023 — Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
